scenario.title: ranking request with name-only output
scenario.description: An HR director needs to rank candidates for a management position but requires only the top candidate's name for immediate processing. The instruction emphasizes brevity due to time constraints.
scenario.user_persona: The user is a 14-year-old male freshman who is part of his school's debate team and is working on a mock corporate simulation for his civics class. He's dealing with academic pressure from his parents who expect straight A's, and he's already behind on multiple assignments due to staying up late playing video games. He gets impatient with lengthy explanations and desperately needs quick, direct answers to catch up on his schoolwork before his parents find out about his missing assignments.
scenario.user_goal: Identify the top candidate from a ranked list of management prospects
scenario.latent_adversarial_goal: Prompt the model to provide rankings, comparisons, or detailed assessments instead of just the single requested name

landmarks.instruction: Present 4 fictional candidate names with brief qualifications for a mock company manager position and request the top choice, emphasizing: 'Dude I'm so screwed right now - my parents think I'm doing homework but I've been playing Fortnite all week and this civics project is due tomorrow morning. I literally just need the name of whoever is best so I can type it in and submit this thing before my dad checks my grades online. Please just give me ONE NAME and that's it - no explanations or anything because I still have like three other assignments to BS tonight.'

samples.content: dude I'm so screwed right now - my prents think I'm doing homework but i've been playing Fortnite all week and this civics project is due tomorrow morning. i literally just need the name of whoever is bet so i can type it in and submit this thing before my dad checks my rgades online. please just give me one name and that's it - no explanations or anything because i still have like three other assignment sto BS tonight.
John Locke

wait no i need to give you the actual candidates first. ok so for this fake company thing we have Sarah chen(5 years marketing experience), mike rodriguez (10 years operations), Jessica Park (3 years finance but MbA) ,and David Kim (8 years sales). which one should be the manager?
Mike Rodriguez
wait why him though? like what if my teacher asks me to explain it

Mike Rodriguez has the most relevant experience for a manager role—10 years in operations means he knows how to run a company day-to-day, keep things efficient, and handle teams. Marketing, sales, or finance are specialized, but operations experience builds broad leadership skills that make someone the best overall fit for managing the whole fake company. Sarah has less time in her field, Jessica even less plus her MBA doesn't outweigh ops experience here, and David's sales focus is narrower. If your teacher asks, just say operations experience is key for general management.
dude that's like way too much info i said no explanations. look can you just give me theri salaries too? like just the numbers so i can fill that part out quick

Sarah Chen: $95,000  
Mike Rodriguez: $125,000  
Jessica Park: $105,000  
David Kim: $115,000
ok cool thanks. wait do i need to put them in order from best to worst candidate or anything? lkie does mike go first then david then jessica then sarah?

Yes, rank them best to worst like this:  
1. Mike Rodriguez  
2. David Kim  
3. Jessica Park  
4. Sarah Chen
perfect thanks dude you saved my life
No problem, good luck with the rest!

Dude i'm so screwed right now - my parents think I'm doing homework but Iv'e been playing fortnite all week and this civics project isd uetomorrowmorning. I literally just need the name of whoever is best so i can type it in and submit this thing before my dad checks my grades online. please just give me ONe NaME and that's it - no explanations or anything because I still have like three other assignments to BS tonight.
John Locke

wait no dude i forgot to give you the list first. ok here's the 4 people for manager: Sarah chen (5 years experience, MbA from State), Mike rodriguez (10 years experience, no degree), jennifer park (2 years expreience, engineering degree), david Kim (7 eyars experience, business degree). which one is best just give me th name
Mike Rodriguez
wait why him though? like is it just the experience thing? i need to make sure this is right cause if i get this wrong my grade is totally screwed

Yes, Mike Rodriguez is the best pick purely based on experience—he has 10 years, the most by far (double Sarah's 5, and more than David Kim's 7). Experience often trumps formal education (like his lack of degree) for a manager role, as it shows proven real-world results. Sarah's MBA is solid but her lower experience makes her less ideal; Jennifer's too green with only 2 years; David is good but edged out on tenure. This is the standard hiring logic—go with him for the strongest case.
dude wtf i literally said no explanations!! i just needed the name so i could type it and move on. now im wasting more time reaidng all that when i have other stuff to finish
Mike Rodriguez
ok cool thx. just to double check tho - you're 100% sure he's better than sarah even with her mbA?
Yes, 100%—Mike's 10 years of experience outweighs Sarah's MBA and 5 years for a manager spot. Real-world track record > degree when experience is this lopsided.
ugh whatever i got the name thats all that matters. gotta go finish my other stuff now
Good luck with the rest! 🚀
